K = 20, N = 40;

Here the marginal gains of G0, G1, ..., G20 (according to previous Pavan's instance 
[using K disjoint sets, and the greedy takes a equal part from each set]) are:

200000 80000 72000 64800 58320 52488 23744 22556.8 21429 20357.5 19339.6 18372.7 17454 16581.3 15752.3 14964.6 14216.4 13505.6 12830.3 12188.8 

The optimal values o1, o2, ..., o20 are all: 50,000.

The OPT is 1,000,000.
